title: Angular Advanced — Paper 2
description: Advanced Angular concepts: reactive forms, custom validators, routing, guards, HTTP client, RxJS, change detection, custom directives/pipes, state management, and testing.

questions.text: What is the difference between setValue() and patchValue() on a FormGroup?

options.text: setValue() requires all controls to be provided; patchValue() allows partial updates
patchValue() requires all controls; setValue() allows partial updates
They are identical in behavior
setValue() only works on FormControl; patchValue() works on FormGroup
form.get('email').errors.required
form.get('email').hasError('required')
form.controls.email.invalid
form.getError('email', 'required')

questions.text: When using FormArray, which method do you call to add a new control at the end of the array?

options.text: formArray.addControl()
formArray.push(new FormControl(''))
formArray.append(new FormControl(''))
formArray.insert(formArray.length, new FormControl(''))
It should use >= instead of <
It returns {} instead of null when valid — validators must return null (not an empty object) to indicate no error
The function name is incorrect
It should accept ValidatorFn as parameter

questions.text: Why would you add debounceTime() inside an async validator?

options.text: To delay showing the error message in the template
To avoid making an HTTP request on every keystroke by waiting until the user pauses typing
To ensure the validator runs after the form submits
To convert the async validator to synchronous

questions.text: When navigating programmatically using Router.navigate(), what option allows you to preserve existing query parameters?

options.text: { keepParams: true }
{ queryParamsHandling: 'preserve' }
{ mergeParams: true }
{ retainQueryParams: true }

questions.text: Which ActivatedRoute property emits new values as query parameters change WITHOUT needing to navigate to a new route?

options.text: this.route.snapshot.queryParams
this.route.queryParams (Observable)
this.router.currentRoute.queryParams
this.route.queryParamMap.snapshot

questions.text: What does the PreloadAllModules strategy do in Angular routing?

options.text: Loads all lazy modules eagerly at startup
After the initial load, preloads all lazy-loaded modules in the background so subsequent navigation is instant
Prevents lazy modules from loading until authenticated
Loads only the currently active module

questions.text: In a functional CanActivateFn guard, which return value allows navigation to proceed?

options.text: false
true
null
undefined
Modifies the response headers
Clones the request and adds an Authorization header before forwarding it
Cancels the request if no token exists
Logs the response body

questions.text: What does the retry(3) operator do when applied to an HTTP Observable?

options.text: Retries the request 3 seconds after failure
Automatically resubscribes to the source up to 3 times on error before propagating the error
Caches the response after 3 successful requests
Emits null up to 3 times before the real value

questions.text: What is the key difference between concatMap and mergeMap?

options.text: concatMap runs all inner observables in parallel; mergeMap runs them sequentially
concatMap waits for each inner observable to complete before subscribing to the next; mergeMap subscribes to all concurrently
concatMap cancels previous observables; mergeMap never cancels
They are aliases for the same operator

questions.text: What does forkJoin([obs1$, obs2$]) emit?

options.text: A value every time either observable emits
A single array of the last values from each observable, emitted when ALL observables complete
The first value from whichever observable emits first
An observable that retries if any source errors

questions.text: What happens when you call markForCheck() on a ChangeDetectorRef in an OnPush component?

options.text: It immediately runs change detection for the component
It marks the component and its ancestors to be checked in the next change detection cycle
It detaches the component from the change detection tree permanently
It resets all form controls in the component

questions.text: What is virtual scrolling in Angular CDK and when should you use it?

options.text: A technique to lazily load images — use it for image-heavy pages
Renders only the DOM items visible in the viewport — use it for large lists (thousands of items) to avoid performance degradation
A CSS trick to hide off-screen elements — use it with hidden attribute
A method to pre-render server-side components
Renderer2 is faster than direct DOM manipulation
Renderer2 abstracts platform-specific DOM access, making the code work in server-side rendering and web workers
Direct nativeElement access is not allowed in Angular
Renderer2 automatically triggers change detection

questions.text: What does ng-content do in an Angular component?

options.text: Defines component styles that are encapsulated
Projects content from the parent component's template into a slot inside the child component's template
Creates a template reference variable
Declares a named router outlet

questions.text: In NgRx, what is the purpose of createAction()?

options.text: Creates a selector for reading state
Creates a typed action creator function with an optional props payload
Creates a reducer case handler
Creates an effect that listens for browser events

questions.text: What is the Angular Signals equivalent of subscribing to an observable for reactive side effects?

options.text: computed()
effect()
toSignal()
signal()

questions.text: What does jasmine.createSpyObj('Service', ['getData']) return in Angular tests?

options.text: An instance of the real Service class with all methods monitored
A fake object with a getData spy method that can have its return value configured
A TestBed provider token
An HttpTestingController mock

questions.text: In an Angular test, what does fixture.detectChanges() do?

options.text: Resets the component to its initial state
Triggers Angular's change detection on the component fixture, updating the DOM
Destroys the component and re-creates it
Compiles the component's template

questions.text: In a child route configuration, what is the significance of the empty path '' with pathMatch: 'full'?

options.text: It matches all routes as a wildcard
It matches only when the URL segment is exactly empty, commonly used to redirect to a default child route
It prevents navigation to child routes
It defines a lazy-loaded module

questions.text: What does the finalize() RxJS operator do in an HTTP request pipeline?

options.text: Retries the request after completion
Executes a callback when the observable completes OR errors, useful for hiding loading spinners
Transforms the final emitted value
Catches and suppresses all errors

questions.text: What is exhaustMap and when would you prefer it over switchMap?

options.text: exhaustMap retries on error; use it for retryable HTTP calls
exhaustMap ignores new outer emissions while an inner observable is active; use it for login buttons to prevent duplicate submissions
exhaustMap merges all inner observables; use it for batch updates
exhaustMap is identical to switchMap but with a different import path
The pipe cannot access component properties
It runs on every change detection cycle regardless of whether the input changed, potentially causing unnecessary recalculations
The pipe will not update when the input string changes
Impure pipes cannot use the slice method

questions.text: What does HttpTestingController.expectOne('/api/users') verify in a unit test?

options.text: That the HTTP response contains exactly one user
That exactly one HTTP request matching the URL was made, and returns a TestRequest to control the mock response
That the component called ngOnInit once
That the route was activated exactly once

questions.text: Which Angular CLI command generates a lazy-loaded feature module with its own routing?

options.text: ng generate module feature
ng generate module feature --route feature --module app.module
ng generate lazy-module feature
ng generate component feature --lazy

questions.text: In Angular, what is XSS and how does Angular's template engine help prevent it by default?

options.text: XSS is cross-site request forgery; Angular adds CSRF tokens automatically
XSS is cross-site scripting; Angular automatically sanitizes values bound with interpolation {{ }} and property bindings to prevent script injection
XSS is extra stylesheet sourcing; Angular bundles all styles to prevent it
XSS is cross-module sharing; Angular's module system prevents unauthorized access

questions.text: In NgRx, when using createEffect with { dispatch: false }, what does this indicate?

options.text: The effect should throw an error instead of dispatching
The effect performs a side effect but does not dispatch any action back to the store
The effect is disabled and will not run
The effect dispatches an action synchronously

questions.text: What is the takeUntilDestroyed() operator used for in Angular?

options.text: It completes an observable after a set number of emissions
It automatically unsubscribes from an observable when the component or service it is used in is destroyed, preventing memory leaks
It destroys the component when the observable errors
It retries the observable until the component is destroyed
